How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-06-18 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Flags tickers hitting 2+ coherent screeners within one trading day, ranking validated combo matches first to surface high-conviction overlap.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

CHPT leads at score 215 with a clean BREAKOUT, MA_CROSS, PULLBACK triple, no mixed signals, though it carries no matched validated combo. RXT at 190 (BREAKOUT, PULLBACK, SMART_MONEY_SURGE) is structurally similar and worth pairing. WDC at 100 stands out for breadth rather than score, four screeners firing including BLOCK_TRADERS and QUANT_RESIDUAL_MOM, which adds an institutional demand angle absent in the pure momentum names. Score distribution is top-heavy: CHPT and RXT are clear outliers above a cluster that runs 99 to 164, and none of the ten names triggered a validated combo (all matched_combo null), which caps conviction - the NEUTRAL macro regime at 52.49 adds no directional lift. First preference is CHPT on score and screener coherence, medium conviction only, given the absence of a VALID combo match.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: MU (score 9, target ~$975.15 (DD20d -11.6%)).

6 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 5 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

Data confidence FULL SIZE · Sharpe 2.39 [CI 1.48-3.33] · N=273 · WR 68% · PF 2.20 · p(Bonf) 0.000 · ½-Kelly 0.18 Full methodology →
🧠 Detects institutional intent via unusual options sweeps confirmed by next-day open-interest delta, EMA8/21 alignment, and low-OI anomaly scoring.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $EFA $TLT
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1EFA ↑80●● Strong$104.51 +0.70%BULLISH SWEEPCALL107$172KOPENING23100PULLBACK1111
2TLT ↑50 Watch$87.13 +0.93%BULLISH SWEEPCALL80$1231KNOT_FOUND—100STRONG1010
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking cannot confirm or contradict the backtest's 68.1% win rate: only 5 of 278 signals have resolved, well short of the 20-trade minimum, and a 100% live win rate on that sample size is statistically meaningless.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEinsufficient data (5 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 278 signals captured · 5 resolved at +20d · WR 100% · avg +200.0% · vs SPY +4.6%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 276 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Thin batch: only 2 signals fired today. EFA is the sole name above the 65 threshold, scoring 80 (Strong tier, bullish sweep), making it the clear top priority. TLT sits at 50 (Watch tier, bullish sweep), 30 points below EFA and below any actionable threshold on its own. Score distribution is bifurcated rather than clustered - one real signal and one bench player. Both are longs, which fits a NEUTRAL macro (52.49) without contradicting it, though simultaneous sweeps in international equity and long duration treasury is an unusual pairing that could reflect broad ETF hedging as easily as directional conviction. EFA first, moderate conviction - Strong tier with confirmed OI delta confirmation, but the overall batch is too lean to force position sizing.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the strategy: 60% win rate and +6.0% average versus SPY's +2.1%, a +3.9-point edge across 500 resolved trades, with May cohort (61% WR, +6.0%) improving on April's softer 53%, and the KEEP verdict stands - though the Watch tier's +7.6% average materially outrunning the Signal tier's +2.4% is the key number worth watching.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 60% · avg +6.0% · beats SPY +2.1% by +3.9 pts

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 501 closed · WR TB 41% · 18% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

PCG at 78 leads a batch that is dominated by utilities and staples - PCG, D, SO, NEE, AEP on the utility side and PEP, CL, PM on the staples side, all Signal tier. The Sinclair +31% utility sector adjustment is doing real work here, pulling these names above the 75 threshold while no ticker clears 80, with 37 of 49 names packed between 65 and 78 - the distribution is clustered and top-heavy but lacks a true standout. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 52.49 does not hurt premium-selling strategies but provides no directional tailwind, and the absence of any pattern data for every pick leaves the trade thesis resting entirely on the vol spread alone. PCG at 78 is the first look, moderate conviction given the null patterns and a macro regime that offers no lift.

Longs tickers in the MID tertile of the variance risk premium (IV vs realized): options priced coherently with realized vol, neither too cheap (LOW = market disbelief) nor too rich (HIGH = catalyst already priced).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

NOMD scores a perfect 100 and RGTI hits 99, both Very strong tier LONG signals, though neither carries a pattern tag so the vol setup is the sole thesis. FITB at 97 adds a large-cap financial to a batch that otherwise skews toward smaller and more volatile names. Score distribution is top-heavy: 13 of 30 signals clear 80, median at 75, no real thin-tail drag. NEUTRAL macro at 52.49 is neither a headwind nor a tailwind, so the screener runs on its own logic today. Lead with NOMD at high conviction given the max score, treat RGTI as a close second with the caveat that quantum-theme vol can gap unpredictably.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is degenerate: all 50 signals print exactly 100, meaning the screener offers zero intra-batch ranking today. ACI and ADSK are the most liquid names in the top picks and carry the full 100 score Very Strong tier LONG read, but with pattern fields null across the board there is no structural differentiator between any of the eight highlighted names. A median of 100 with every signal at or above 80 is a ceiling-pile, not a signal, and it tells you the put-vol threshold was tripped broadly rather than selectively. NEUTRAL macro at 52.49 adds no tailwind. Conviction is low across the board; if forced to act, ADSK is the most institutionally liquid venue to express this thesis, but size down until the screener produces a dispersed distribution again.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape contradicts the backtest in the most important place: the Watch tier (333 trades, 64% WR, +2.64% avg) is carrying the entire 58% headline win rate, while Strong and Very strong tiers are near-zero or zero winners, and the monthly cohort trend shows a clear deceleration from March's 66.8% WR and +4.9% avg down to May's 46.4% and +0.65%, confirming the screener's edge is narrowing and currently concentrated in names the scoring model ranks lowest.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 491 closed · WR TB 42% · 16% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is unusually compressed: all 50 signals clear 80, median sits at 85, and seven names - ACRS, DIOD, ENS, FLYW, LGN, MCHP, STAA - print at the maximum 95 with AAON just behind at 90. That top-heaviness sounds bullish until you cross it against the 90-day tier breakdown: the Very strong cohort carries a 0% win rate and -10.8% average return, the worst performing tier in the entire tracking universe. Today's batch maps almost entirely into that dead zone. Neutral macro (52.49) adds no tailwind. If anything is worth watching here, MCHP and DIOD are more liquid semi names where a miss is smaller, but conviction is low. Do not press the 95-scorers - let this batch breathe and wait for Watch-tier entries where the tape has actually delivered.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

FIG and RR co-top the batch at 94, both flagged Very strong with no pattern context available, which limits single-name conviction and keeps this squarely a basket read. CRCL at 92 is next, followed by a dense cluster of ACN, BCO, CTSH, and FDP between 90 and 91. Score distribution is compressed and top-heavy: all 32 signals clear 80, median sits at 85, and the range from floor to ceiling is only 6 points, suggesting the screener is not discriminating sharply within today's batch. Macro is NEUTRAL at 52.49, which is not a hostile environment for mean-reversion but provides no directional tailwind either. The actionable read is a small equal-weight basket in LEAPS across the top tier, with FIG as the first allocation given its rank, at low per-name conviction consistent with the 1-2% sizing the backtest demands.

Data confidence FULL SIZE VVIX climax V3 STRICT_VALID, P(VIX spike) 11%->4% in 30d, wired prod (lib/vvix_climax_detector) Full methodology →
🧠 Flags vol-of-vol climax by combining VVIX percentile, VIX term structure, FOMC proximity, and weekend effect to time sell-volatility entries at peak fear.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑61 Watch$745.88 +0.66%SELL VOL1.33
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 Detects informed positioning by screening options volume over 5x the 20-day mean, premium above $50K, and Vol/OI above 5, filtered to calm non-extended stocks only.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape shows 100% win rate and a +188.6 percentage-point beat over SPY across all resolved tiers, which confirms the backtest profit factor of 2.16, but only 24 of 620 signals have resolved, making the tracking too thin to treat as independent validation of the marginal backtest verdict.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AAOI is the only name at score 100 and Very Strong tier, but the signal is internally conflicted: the direction flags LONG while the pattern is a BEARISH SWEEP, which means the flow itself is bearish and the directional tag needs scrutiny before acting. COIN at 78 and TLT at 77 are cleaner, both Signal tier with bullish sweeps aligned to their LONG direction. The score distribution is thin: 15 signals, median 58, just one name above 80 and four above 65, so the batch is top-heavy with a weak middle. A neutral macro regime at 52.49 does not add tailwind or headwind. First look goes to COIN at medium conviction, given the clean sweep alignment and sector momentum; hold AAOI until the direction-pattern conflict resolves intraday.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLU leads at 95 (Very Strong) - utilities topping a sector model in a NEUTRAL macro (52.49) is a defensive rotation signal, not a broad risk-on read. XLC and XLE both score 80 (Strong), pairing a growth-adjacent sector with an energy name that benefits from any commodity bid. Score distribution is top-heavy and gapped: four names at 80 or above, then a hard drop to a cluster of 55s, with the median at 60 and nothing in the 65-79 range except XLI at 65. That gap weakens the case for broad sector rotation. The NEUTRAL regime neither validates nor disqualifies the defensive leadership, but utilities and energy printing together with tech (XLK) stuck at 55 Watch suggests the tape is rotating away from growth, not toward it. First preference is XLU on highest score and clearest pattern confirmation, at moderate conviction - do not size as a risk-on trade.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape posts 50% WR and +3.7% avg versus SPY +0.77%, a WATCH verdict that monthly cohorts partly support as WR has recovered from January's 29% and -9.5% avg to April's 53% and +5.2% avg, but the most important number is the tier inversion - Strong names run only 29% WR at -7.4% avg while Signal names run 68% WR at +10.5% avg, which combined with a p-Bonferroni of 0.60 on the backtest means the structural edge here is fragile and sizing must reflect that.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 491 closed · WR TB 35% · 9% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

OCS scores a perfect 100 and leads the batch as a Very Strong BREAKOUT, followed by ADCT and WW both at 95 with the same pattern, making these three the clearest setups today. The score distribution is technically broad in that all 50 signals clear 65, but only 14 reach 80-plus and the median sits at 71, so the bulk of the batch is middling filler just above the entry threshold. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 52.49 neither supports nor undermines a capitulation bounce thesis, though this setup class prefers a stabilising tape over a trending one, and NEUTRAL is an acceptable backdrop. Start with OCS at high conviction given the perfect score and Very Strong tier designation; ADCT and WW are credible secondary names but size both down for small-cap liquidity risk.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Ranks equities by annualized exponential-regression slope times R-squared over 90 days, active only when SPY trades above its 200-day moving average.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Catches stocks sustaining post-earnings gap-ups via EPS surprise magnitude, gap retention, and analyst revision momentum over a 1-3 week hold.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $BBCP $BKE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1BBCP ↑90●●● Very strong$10.94 -0.41%500.0%437.6%
2BKE ↑70● Signal$44.95 +1.77%24.7%4-3.7%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape largely confirms viability: 61% win rate and avg +3.6% over 494 resolved trades beats SPY by 3.5 points, a number strong enough to sustain the KEEP verdict even without a rigorous backtest Sharpe on file, and the May 2026 cohort (60.2% WR, avg +3.78% on 512 signals) runs in the same band.

Two signals only, which is a thin batch. BBCP scores 90 (Very strong, PRIMED) and is the clear first choice given it sits at the top of today's range and hits the highest tier. 🧠 Scans for 5-day post-earnings price continuation using short-term momentum and relative volume to capture PEAD drift in the first week after a surprise.
Construction
Tactical 5-day post-earnings continuation: scans for follow-through bars 1-5 days after surprise. Score from short-term momentum + relative volume.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Recent earnings with EPS surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Price ≥ $5, volume ≥ 500K, market cap ≥ $500M

Score
min(100, surprise_pct × 4). LONG.

Data confidence HALF SIZE backtest_murphy_intermarket doctrine 8-step: 13 buckets MARGINAL_INVERSE Bonferroni-significant (+6 to +14% SPY at 60-120d after intermarket correlation breaks) Full methodology →
🧠 Fires a contrarian SPY long when two or more major intermarket correlations break by 2 sigma over 7 days, betting on dislocation mean-reversion.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 50 signals score exactly 100 with zero dispersion, median equals max, making internal ranking impossible - the screener is binary today, not ordinal. APD is the standout name: a large-cap industrial crossing above the 200-day EMA with weekly confirmation carries more structural weight than the cluster of micro-cap biotechs (ABOS, ALLO, AVIR, AVTX, AVTX) that dominate the rest of the list. Pattern fields are null across every pick, stripping out whatever additional context the scoring model would normally provide. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (52.49), which neither supports nor kills a momentum-restoration trade, but does nothing to resolve the noise problem created by a batch this undifferentiated. If forced to act, APD first at low conviction - large-cap trend restorations on this setup carry more follow-through history than a biotech squeeze batch with no pattern confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The aggregate 90-day tape supports keeping this screener - 59% WR and +4.7% avg beat SPY by 4.7 points - but the tier breakdown inverts expectations so sharply that the headline numbers obscure real risk: Watch-tier names (64.5% WR, +7.1% avg) dramatically outperform Very strong (30% WR, -11.2%), and the May cohort recovery to +2.6% avg after April's -0.73% is directionally positive but not yet conclusive.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 496 closed · WR TB 36% · 24% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AI scores highest at 95 and BEAM, CHH, DDD all sit at 90, all carrying the Very strong tier and the PRIMED pattern, which sounds compelling until you check the tier breakdown: Very strong names have delivered a 30% win rate and avg -11.2% over 20 days across 90-day tracking, the worst of any tier by a wide margin. CAKE and CBRL at 85 in the Strong tier are the more actionable names, given Strong posts 53% WR and +4.2% avg. The score distribution is broadly clustered high, with 47 of 50 signals above 65 and 16 above 80, suggesting a crowded signal day rather than a selective one. Macro is NEUTRAL at 52.49, neither a tailwind nor a drag for squeeze setups. First preference is CAKE at moderate conviction, avoiding the Very strong tier entirely given its documented underperformance.

🧠 Flags stocks with abnormal volume surges across three lookback windows multiplied by multi-horizon price momentum, surfacing names where buying pressure and price trend are reinforcing simultaneously.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The batch is top-heavy in a way that makes ranking nearly useless: all 50 signals score 96 or above, with 50 of 50 clearing the 80 threshold, so the score distribution is fully compressed at the ceiling. Among the four names tied at 100, MRVL and AMAT stand out as the most coherent picks given semiconductor volume leadership tends to cluster around real demand signals, not noise. DELL and WDC offer storage-cycle confirmation if you read them as correlated. MRNA at 100 is notable but pattern data is null across the board, so there is no structural read beyond raw volume-momentum coincidence. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 52.49 does not suppress the trade but does not endorse urgency either. First preference is MRVL at highest conviction given its sector alignment with the broader semi tape, treated as a starter position only until pattern context or a catalyst materializes.

🧠 Detects institutional accumulation by flagging large OTM options blocks over $200K premium with 1-10 DTE on low-activity stocks, confirmed via next-day OI delta to filter closing flow.
Construction
Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape confirms the screener is earning its keep - 61.3% WR and +5.1% avg over 20 days against SPY's +0.82% is a clean +4.3 point edge - but the monthly cohorts show avg return compressing from +9.1% in April to +4.7% in May, a deterioration worth watching even as WR held above 55% in both months.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

PINK tops the batch at score 84 (Strong, LONG) with no pattern label provided, so the conviction rests entirely on the premium and OI metrics behind the score. SMQ at 82 and the KW/VSCO pair at 81 form a tight Strong-tier cluster, all directionally LONG and all without pattern context - data is thin on the qualitative side. The distribution is narrow at the top: only 4 of 32 signals clear 80, median sits at 62, and 14 reach 65 or above, meaning most of today's batch is mid-grade filler rather than a broad high-conviction sweep. Macro is NEUTRAL at 52.49, which removes a tailwind but does not contradict the setup. Take PINK first at moderate conviction, sized below full given the absent pattern context and a neutral tape.

🧠 Detects excess high bars at the top of the value area with rejection tails at least 1.8x ATR and location at or above 70 percent, flagging likely short reversals.
Construction
Mirror of DALTON: excess high bars at the top of the value area, rejection tail >= 1.8x ATR, location >= 70%.
Top picks: $FEMR $DGS
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) in the last 3 bars
  • SHORT direction: excess on the HIGH side AND trade location ≥ 70%
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). SHORT.

🧠 Detects institutional rejection tails at Market Profile value-area extremes, exploiting mean reversion when price overextends beyond accepted value by 1.8x ATR.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $E
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1E ↑100●●● Very strong$48.96 -1.57%LOW3.3526.5%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With only 2 resolved trades showing 100% win rate and +6.76% avg return versus SPY at +1.10%, the live tape looks encouraging but is statistically meaningless, and the WATCH verdict reflects exactly that, as the threshold for any confidence is 20 resolved trades, which this screener has not approached.

Data confidence STARTER PEAD gap-up + weekly trigger, small N, mechanically sound but too early for a verdict Full methodology →
🧠 Catches post-earnings drift continuation by entering on the first weekly reclaim above a prior red-candle pullback within four weeks of a gap-up.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.
Top picks: $ASMG
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayGapVol ratioBars ago
1ASMG ↑100●●● Very strong$61.87 +5.17%9.5%1.990
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Opening gap on one of the last 3 bars: 5.0% ≤ gap < 10.0%
  • Volume confirmation: gap-day volume / 20d average > 1.5
  • Dollar-volume ≥ $50M
Score
gap_pct × 15 (not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled, top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 Filters SMART_FLOW signals by skipping entries when KVO or Force Index volume indicators flip within a 3-day window, using volume conflict as a negative gate.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).
Top picks: $MCHP
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1MCHP ↑50 Watch$98.37 +4.53%BULLISH SWEEPCALL105$130KNEUTRAL08555
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is a single name, MCHP, scoring 50 on a BULLISH SWEEP pattern at Watch tier, which sits at the bottom of any actionable range given zero signals above 65 and zero above 80. The distribution is not a distribution at all - one signal at the median floor means the filter is rejecting nearly everything, which is the design working as intended rather than a broken screener. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 52.49 offers no tailwind or headwind to rationalize stretching conviction here. MCHP is the only candidate but at score 50 it is a monitor-only name, not a trigger.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.

The distribution is top-heavy in a narrow way: 34 of 50 signals cluster at 65 or above with a median of 65, meaning most of the batch is Signal-tier noise rather than high-conviction setup. Macro is NEUTRAL at 52.49, which provides no tailwind for aggressive shorting. LPRO and VELO get first look on score and pattern alone, but conviction must stay low: the backtest is formally rejected and the regime does not favor the thesis.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is completely ceiling-clipped: all 50 names sit between 99 and 100, median 99, so the ranker offers zero within-batch differentiation today. Among the named picks, AMD and INTC are the highest-liquidity semis at score 100, while ALAB is the purer AI-infrastructure angle at the same tier with a smaller float that can move faster. STX and WDC add a storage-capex read that has tracked AI buildout spending, though no pattern data exists for any pick to sharpen the thesis. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 52.49 provides no directional tailwind, and a semiconductor-heavy batch in a flat-macro tape is a coin flip. AMD is the first preference on liquidity and sector momentum clarity, but conviction is low given the score compression and the screener's rejected backtest.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape confirms the KILL verdict - WR of 74% sounds fine but average return of +5.2% trails SPY by 2.3 points, and monthly cohorts show average return deteriorating from +6.7% in March to +1.6% in May, so both the live results and the trend run against the backtest edge.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SR at 75 is the only TRIGGER in the batch, meaning it has active daily crossover confirmation rather than just setup positioning, which makes it the lone name worth prioritizing. AIV at 72 and DKL at 66 both sit in Signal tier but remain in SETUP phase, so the reversal signal has not fired yet. Score distribution is thin: median 53, nothing clears 80, and only 3 names reach 65, which is a weak batch by any measure. NEUTRAL macro at 52.49 is neither a tailwind nor a headwind for contrarian plays, so the tape itself is not the problem. SR first on any interest, but at low conviction given the screener carries a KILL verdict and the batch has no high-conviction scores.

🧠 Detects unusual volume, OI delta, and premium on crypto-linked ETFs to front-run directional momentum in spot crypto proxies like BITO, IBIT, and GBTC.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The headline KEEP verdict (65% WR, +2.0% avg, +1.7 pts over SPY) is entirely carried by the Watch tier (48 trades, 72.9% WR, +4.87% avg), while the Strong and Signal tiers produced -13.2% and -18.1% respectively across just 7 resolved trades, confirming the backtest REJECT and signaling that elevated flow scores are negatively predictive at high conviction in the post-spot-ETF regime.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

IREN leads at 90 (Very Strong, TRIGGER) and is the only name in today's six-signal batch at that score level, making it the cleanest expression of the thesis. CLSK and MARA both print 85 (Strong, TRIGGER) and represent the liquid miners most tightly coupled to BTC flow. The batch is top-heavy: four of six names score 80 or above, median 80, with only CORZ at 55 dragging the tail. NEUTRAL macro (52.49) is neither a tailwind nor a headwind for crypto proxies specifically. However, the tier-level tracking data creates a serious problem here: Strong-tier trades have averaged -13.2% and Signal-tier trades -18.1% over the past 90 days, meaning the highest-conviction labels in this screener have historically destroyed capital. Conviction is capped at Watch. If forced to act, IREN is first, but sizing stays small and the stop is tight.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AIQ leads at 77 and BLOK sits at 76, making crypto-adjacent and AI infrastructure the two clearest signals in this batch. BITQ at 73 extends the crypto theme, so the top three are thematically concentrated rather than diversified across the broader emerging-theme universe. The distribution is soft: max is only 77, zero names clear 80, and just 12 of 50 cross 65, which puts the field in a narrow, middling band. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 52.49 does not provide a tailwind, so high-conviction positioning is hard to justify. If forced to pick one, AIQ first, low conviction.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 8 top picks land at score 100, Very strong tier, LONG, with no pattern metadata attached, so differentiation within the batch is thin. BL and CELH are the most liquid names here and carry the full 100 score, but the 90-day tier data is a caution: Very strong picks have averaged only 3.4% at 58% win rate, well below the Watch tier's 11.4% average at 63%. The score distribution is top-heavy, with 12 of 50 signals at 80 or above and the median at 70, suggesting a reasonably concentrated signal day rather than a diluted sweep. Macro is neutral at 52.49, neither a tailwind nor a headwind. Given that Watch-tier names have historically generated the bulk of the alpha, preference is for any Watch-tier names that clear 80 on score, and within today's Very strong batch, BL first at reduced size given the tier's historically muted return profile.

🧠 Aggregates quarterly 13F filings from 20 hedge funds, scoring new and increased positions to surface concentrated institutional accumulation.
Construction
Quarterly SEC 13F-HR filings from 20 top hedge funds via EDGAR. Whale Score: +20/NEW (cap 60), +10/INCREASED (cap 30), -5/CLOSED, -3/DECREASED. Stale 60-90 days by nature (quarterly).

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ASML at 97 and FB at 93 are the only Very Strong reads in a 50-signal batch where the median sits at 55, meaning conviction is narrow and concentrated at the top. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 52.49, which neither amplifies nor penalizes a long bias here, but the 13F staleness (signals are 60-90 days old by construction) means price has likely already moved on the catalyst. ASML is the first name to size, given the highest score in the batch, with medium conviction given the data lag and absence of pattern confirmation.

🧠 Identifies individual stocks lagging within LATE_CHASE or RIDING sectors, scoring by the product of sector relative strength and stock under-performance to isolate catch-up candidates.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

Today's batch is weak. All 17 signals scored at exactly 50, the floor, with zero names at or above 65, meaning the screener found no genuinely differentiated catch-up setups. Among the eight listed picks, KTOS and CF carry the most recognizable sector narratives (defense momentum and ag-chemicals respectively), but their scores are identical to HYMC and LXU, so there is no quantitative basis to rank them higher. The score distribution is not clustered around a high-quality core, it is flatlined at the minimum, which is a sign of a noisy or thin signal day rather than a rich opportunity set. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 52.49 does not disqualify a sector catch-up thesis, but it offers no tailwind either. Conviction is low across the board and no name earns a first look today.

🧠 Screens for the cheapest US equities by EV/EBIT (Acquirer's Multiple), targeting names where operating earnings are large relative to total enterprise cost.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape is completely unreadable - 0 of 21 positions have resolved against the 20-trade minimum required for any verdict, no backtest exists, and monthly cohorts are empty, so there is no number to confirm or contradict anything.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BZ leads at a perfect score of 100, making it the cheapest name in today's universe by the multiple, and WRD at 97 is a near-equivalent pairing worth running alongside it. KROS at 93 is an eyebrow-raiser - a clinical-stage biotech surfacing near the top of an operating-earnings screen demands a hard look at the EBIT figure before acting, as one-time items or unusual accounting can game this metric for that sector. The score distribution is top-heavy: 7 of 16 names clear 80 and the median sits at 73, so this is not a thin or desperate batch, there is genuine spread in the cheapness. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 52.49 is not a tailwind for deep value - this style needs either a catalyst or patience - so overall conviction is moderate. Start with BZ at the highest conviction, size conservatively, and treat KROS as a secondary research item until the EBIT source is verified.

🧠 Detects Form 4 cluster buys where 3+ insiders buy within 30 days, scoring by rank, dollar size, and recency to flag undisclosed conviction.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape supports the screener with WR 64% and avg +4.0% beating SPY's +1.7% by 2.3 points, but the May-to-April cohort swing from WR 71% down to 48% flags meaningful regime sensitivity that a screener with no formal backtest cannot yet explain.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 64% · avg +4.0% · beats SPY +1.7% by +2.3 pts
Last 90 days: 476 signals captured · 251 resolved at +20d · WR 64% · avg +4.0% · vs SPY +1.7%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 263 closed · WR TB 30% · 51% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=17 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags retail euphoria via call spike, Reddit surge, and parabolic price stretch to identify short entries when crowd momentum peaks without an earnings catalyst.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $ACN $AOS $ASX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg
1ACN ↓100●●● Very strong$132.43 -15.12%FOMO1.37-15.1%
2AOS ↓100●●● Very strong$59.13 +2.16%FOMO1.572.2%
3ASX ↓100●●● Very strong$39.31 +4.99%FOMO4.085.0%
4ARES ↓98●●● Very strong$130.49 +1.67%EUPHORIA0.981.7%
5BANC ↓68● Signal$20.12 +1.46%GREED0.681.5%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The backtest is not yet valid (N=17, below the 30-trade minimum), so the 90d live tape is the only evidence base: WR 67% and avg +2.5% vs SPY +0.4% support a KEEP, but the April 2026 cohort (WR 17%, avg -15.9% on 6 names) versus May's 73% WR and +4.5% avg confirms this screener is sharply regime-dependent, with the key number being that April drawdown of -15.9% average per signal.

🧠 Identifies highest relative-strength tickers inside leading sectors, combining 20-day RS vs SPY, sector breadth, and cluster rank into a single score.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $AAT $AAMI $ABCL
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1AAT ↑82●● Strong$24.09 +1.03%Real Estate19.0%31.5%
2AAMI ↑76● Signal$73.00 -11.75%Financials9.2%52.1%
3ABCL ↑74● Signal$5.58 +2.57%Healthcare7.8%47.3%
4A ↑65● Signal$125.60 +1.02%Healthcare15.9%17.1%

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape (51 resolved, 57% WR, avg +4.6% vs SPY -1.0%) and the single May 2026 cohort (57.4% WR, avg +4.6%) are in close agreement, which is a clean signal the edge is real and stable - but the key number is the Signal-tier split at +11.7% avg, which is doing all the heavy lifting while the top-scored tiers drag.

EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects bearish put/call skew spikes vs 90-day baseline as a contrarian long signal when the underlying remains calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $ORCL $MU $COST
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1ORCL ↑81●● Strong$178.46 -2.77%55%40-0.06BUY CALL (call skew che…
2MU ↑58 Watch$1102.49 +5.68%84%60—BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
3COST ↓55 Watch$957.13 -0.88%56%77—SELL CALL/PUT (IV rich)
4AVGO ↑53 Watch$409.75 +4.29%46%520.03BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the screener belongs: 61% win rate, +13.9% average, beating SPY by 11.4 points, and May 2026 strengthened further to 64.5% and +17.8% versus April's softer 47% and +9.2%, with the key number being the Watch-tier 79% win rate that makes the signal-strength inversion the primary filter to apply.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Four signals today, thin batch. ORCL is the only name above 80 (score 81, Strong tier, LONG), but the tier data cuts against it: Strong-tier signals in the 90-day window ran a 22% win rate and averaged -2.3%, the worst of any tier. MU (score 58, Watch, LONG) and COST (score 55, Watch, SHORT) sit in the Watch tier, which historically delivered 79% win rate and +22.6% average - the performance inversion across tiers is the defining feature of this screener. The median score of 55 with only one name above 65 makes today a low-conviction batch, fitting the NEUTRAL macro at 52.49. First preference is MU at low size, Watch-tier only; skip ORCL despite its score.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks where IV30 exceeds HV20 by more than 1.5 standard deviations on a 90-day lookback, surfacing inflated fear premiums ripe for short-vol trades.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $NFLX $NKE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1NFLX ↓69● Signal$76.52 -0.57%41%24%1.73FEAR
2NKE ↓62 Watch$44.45 +0.59%53%34%1.56FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the screener earns its keep - 176 resolved trades show a 55.7% win rate and +7.2% avg return vs. SPY's +3.3%, a +4.0-point edge, and April and May cohorts both hold near 55-56% WR, though April's +10.8% avg against May's +5.4% suggests return magnitude depends heavily on the regime in play.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects fake breakouts above the value area high that close back inside, trapping longs and generating a short signal.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $ACCO $ARMK
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1ACCO ↓65● Signal$3.85 +0.00%BULL_TRAP4.042.96
2ARMK ↓65● Signal$53.59 +0.20%BULL_TRAP54.6345.34
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 One resolved trade, a 0.0% win rate, and an average return of -8.42% against SPY's +0.99% is a single losing data point with empty monthly cohorts, so the backtest verdict of NOT_YET_BACKTESTED remains the only honest characterization of this screener's edge.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects stocks where put IV dominates call IV above the 90th percentile, flagging unusual directional put demand or institutional hedging activity.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$409.75 +4.29%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape looks clean on the surface - 6 resolved trades, 100% win rate, avg 20d return of 18.8% against SPY's 1.9% - but 6 resolved trades against the 20-trade minimum is statistical noise, not evidence, and the WATCH verdict stands until the dataset grows.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags pre-earnings names where straddle IV underprices the expected move implied by Yang-Zhang 20-day historical vol, targeting long-vol entry.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $ACN
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1ACN ↑50 Watch$132.43 -15.12%REVERSAL0UP (reversal)BUY CALL (reversal)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is one name: ACN at a score of 50, Watch tier, no pattern flagged. A single signal at the floor of the Watch band is not a batch, it is noise. The score distribution is a single point at 50 with zero names above 65, so there is no top-heavy cluster or actionable skew to characterize. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (52.49), which neither supports nor penalizes a long-vol bias. Do not act on ACN here. Wait for at least a second confirming signal or a score above 65 before sizing any position.

Bull/Bear Traps (Value Area)

TRAPEOD
Live: trap_screener.py (LONG leg = bear traps)

sizing guide, not permission

HALF SIZE

Refresh
EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects bear and bull traps by flagging closes outside the value area (VAL/VAH) that fully reclaim the range the next session, signaling trapped directional participants.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $AGG
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1AGG ↑60 Watch$99.00 +0.40%BEAR_TRAP98.8999.080.37
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape shows 75% win rate and a 23.4% avg 20-day return vs a 0.62% SPY baseline, which looks striking, but with only 4 resolved trades against a 20-trade minimum the verdict is correctly held at WATCH and no statistical weight can be attached to those numbers.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEinsufficient data (4 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 61 signals captured · 4 resolved at +20d · WR 75% · avg +23.4% · vs SPY +0.6%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 22 closed · WR TB 45% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=2 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects volatility mispricing via composite of IV rank, IV30/HV20 ratio, skew, term structure slope, and options flow, flagging tradeable vol dislocations.
Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.
Top picks: $VXX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1VXX ↓85●● Strong$22.85 -2.93%56%331%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape with 14 resolved trades shows a 21.4% win rate and a mean outcome of -10.25%, against an SPY baseline of +0.23%, contradicting any edge thesis, though the verdict is explicitly WATCH due to insufficient sample size, and the May 2026 cohort's -8.53% average with a 25% win rate offers no improvement over April's single-trade disaster at -24.6%.

EOD
Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 ALTMAN screens US equities for financial distress risk using Altman Z-score methodology to flag deteriorating credit quality or recovery candidates.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).

Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

🧠 VSA flags institutional accumulation or distribution by cross-referencing price spread width, closing position, and volume against prior bars.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.

Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
